42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.

52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |

62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |

67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |

70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

8	/-!
9	# Grassmannian affine charts
10	
11	This file contains the single blueprint-pinned declaration for the affine
12	charts of the Grassmannian scheme: `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart`.
13	
14	For `I ⊆ Fin r` with `#I = d`, the affine chart `U^I` of the Grassmannian
15	`Gr(d, r)` is the spectrum of the polynomial ring in the `d(r-d)` free matrix
16	entries — `Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{q ∉ I}` — which is non-canonically isomorphic
17	to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
18	
19	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_affine_chart`
20	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`).
21	-/

25	universe u

27	open AlgebraicGeometry

29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

31	/- Blueprint: def:gr_affine_chart (chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex) -/
32	
33	/- Planner note:
34	   Blueprint `def:gr_affine_chart` (Nitsure §1, "Construction by gluing together
35	   affine patches"): for `I ⊆ {1..r}` with `#I = d`, `X^I` is the `d×r` matrix
36	   whose `I`-minor is the `d×d` identity and whose other `d(r-d)` entries are
37	   independent indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` over ℤ. The affine chart is
38	   `U^I := Spec ℤ[X^I] = Spec (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)` —
39	   the spectrum of the polynomial ring on the `d(r-d)` free entries; non-canonically
40	   `≅ 𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`. The prover should build `affineChart` as
41	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Spec` of that `CommRingCat` (the MvPolynomial ring on the
42	   free-entry index type), via
43	   `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` or an equivalent index of cardinality
44	   `d(r-d)`. -/
45	
46	/-- The **affine chart** `U^I` of the Grassmannian `Gr(d, r)` indexed by a
47	`d`-element subset `I : Finset (Fin r)`.
48	
49	Concretely `U^I = Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{p : Fin d, q ∉ I}`, the spectrum of the
50	polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ` in the `d(r-d)`
51	free entries of the standard matrix representative with `I`-minor equal to the
52	identity. This chart is non-canonically isomorphic to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
53	
54	The Grassmannian scheme is obtained by gluing the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts
55	`U^I` along the Plücker cocycle transition maps. -/
56	noncomputable def affineChart (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) : Scheme :=
57	  AlgebraicGeometry.Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))
58	
59	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Grassmannian transition maps
60	
61	The transition map `θ_{I,J}` of `def:gr_transition` is a Cramer-inverse computation
62	over the localised chart ring `R^I_J := ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`, followed by the universal
63	property of the away-localisation. We build it bottom-up over the chart ring
64	`R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, all elementary matrix algebra
65	over `ℤ`. The `d`-subset combinatorics are handled by the order isomorphism
66	`Finset.orderIsoOfFin : I.card = d → (Fin d ≃o ↥I)`.
67	
68	Blueprint references: `def:gr_universal_matrix`, `def:gr_minor_det`,
69	`def:gr_universal_minor`, `lem:gr_minorDet_unit`, `def:gr_universalMinorInv`,
70	`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`, `def:gr_image_matrix`, `def:gr_transition_pre`
71	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
72	
73	/-- The **universal matrix** `X^I` (`def:gr_universal_matrix`): the `d × r` matrix
74	over the chart ring `R^I = MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` whose `I`-minor is the
75	`d × d` identity (read through the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥I`) and whose remaining
76	`d(r-d)` entries are the free indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` (`q ∉ I`). Project-local:
77	the universal point of the affine chart `affineChart d r I`. -/
78	noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
79	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
80	  fun p q =>
81	    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
82	    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)
83	
84	/-- The **minor determinant** `P^I_J = det(X^I_J)` (`def:gr_minor_det`): the determinant
85	of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose columns are those indexed by
86	`J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥J`. Project-local: defines the
87	principal open `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`. -/
88	noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
89	    (hJ : J.card = d) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
90	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
91	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
92	
93	/-- The **localised `J`-minor** `X^I_J` over `R^I_J` (`def:gr_universal_minor`): the
94	`J`-minor of `universalMatrix d r I` with entries pushed forward along the structure map
95	`R^I → R^I_J = Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`. Project-local. -/
96	noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
97	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
98	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
99	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
100	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
101	
102	/-- The localised minor determinant is a unit (`lem:gr_minorDet_unit`): `det(X^I_J)` is the
103	image of `P^I_J` under the away-localisation structure map, hence a unit. Project-local. -/
104	theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
105	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
106	  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
107	      = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
108	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
109	  rw [h]
110	  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
111	
112	/-- The **Cramer inverse** `(X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_universalMinorInv`): the Mathlib
113	nonsingular inverse of the localised `J`-minor. Its entries lie in `R^I_J` by Cramer's
114	rule. Project-local. -/
115	noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
116	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
117	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
118	  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
119	
120	/-- The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse (`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`):
121	since `det(X^I_J)` is a unit, `(X^I_J)⁻¹` is a genuine left and right inverse.
122	Project-local. -/
123	theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
124	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
125	    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
126	    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
127	  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
128	   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
129	
130	/-- The **image matrix** `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`def:gr_image_matrix`): the product of the
131	Cramer inverse with the universal matrix base-changed to `R^I_J`. Its entries are the
132	prospective images of the indeterminates `x^J_{p,q}` under `θ_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
133	noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
134	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
135	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
136	  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
137	
138	/-- The **pre-localisation hom** `θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (`def:gr_transition_pre`): the
139	`ℤ`-algebra map out of the chart ring of `J` sending each free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}`
140	to the `(p,q)`-entry of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
141	noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
142	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
143	    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ]
144	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
145	  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)
146	
147	/-- The `I`-minor of the universal matrix `X^I` is the `d × d` identity (`P^I_I = 1`
148	underlies `lem:gr_transition_self`): the `I`-columns of `X^I` read through the order iso
149	`Fin d ≃o ↥I` form the identity block. Project-local. -/
150	theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
151	    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
152	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
153	  ext p p'
154	  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
155	  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
156	  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
157	    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
158	  by_cases h : p = p'
159	  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
160	  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]
161	
162	/-- Submatrix on the columns of a right matrix factor commutes with the product
163	(rows reindexed by `id`). Project-local helper: avoids a matrix-multiplication
164	instance-keying issue that blocks the generic `rw` of Mathlib's submatrix-mul lemmas. -/
165	private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
166	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
167	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
168	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
169	
170	/-- The `J`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the identity: `M_J =
171	(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_J = 1` (`def:gr_image_matrix`, used in the cocycle/transition arguments).
172	Project-local. -/
173	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
174	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
175	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
176	      (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
177	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
178	        (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
179	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
180	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
181	          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
182	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
183	  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
184	
185	/-- The `I`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the Cramer inverse:
186	`M_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
187	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
188	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
189	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
190	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
191	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
192	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
193	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
194	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
195	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
196	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
197	    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]
198	
199	/-- The pre-localisation hom realises the matrix formula `θ̃_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`:
200	applying `θ̃_{I,J}` entrywise to the universal matrix `X^J` yields the image matrix `M`
201	(`def:gr_transition_pre`, `def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
202	theorem universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
204	    (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
205	      = imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ := by
206	  ext p q
207	  simp only [Matrix.map_apply, universalMatrix]
208	  by_cases hq : q ∈ J
209	  · rw [dif_pos hq]
210	    set k := (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ).symm ⟨q, hq⟩ with hk
211	    have hqk : (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) = q := by simp [hk]
212	    have himg : imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ p q = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) _) p k := by
213	      have e := congrFun (congrFun (imageMatrix_submatrix_self d r I J hI hJ) p) k
214	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq] at e
215	      rw [← hqk]; exact e
216	    rw [himg, Matrix.one_apply, apply_ite (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ), map_one, map_zero]
217	    have hcond : ((J.orderIsoOfFin hJ p : Fin r) = q) ↔ (p = k) := by
218	      conv_lhs => rw [← hqk]
219	      rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
220	    by_cases hpk : p = k
221	    · rw [if_pos (hcond.mpr hpk), if_pos hpk]
222	    · rw [if_neg (hcond.not.mpr hpk), if_neg hpk]
223	  · rw [dif_neg hq, transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X]
224	
225	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to a unit (`lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`):
226	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) = det((X^I_J)⁻¹) = det(X^I_J)⁻¹ = 1/P^I_J`, a unit of `R^I_J`. This is the
227	hypothesis that lets `θ̃_{I,J}` extend along the away-localisation `R^J[1/P^J_I]`.
228	Project-local. -/
229	theorem isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
230	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)) := by
231	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
232	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
233	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
234	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
235	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
236	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
237	  have hmul : (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).det * (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = 1 := by
238	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
239	  exact IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one _ hmul
240	
241	/-- The **transition map** `θ_{I,J} : R^J[1/P^J_I] → R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`def:gr_transition`):
242	the away-localisation lift of the pre-hom `θ̃_{I,J}` along `P^J_I`, available because
243	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is a unit (`isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`). It is the comorphism of
244	the chart-overlap isomorphism `U^I_J → U^J_I`. Project-local. -/
245	noncomputable def transitionMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
246	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
247	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) →+* Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
248	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
249	    (g := (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
250	    (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
251	
252	/-- `θ_{I,I}` is the identity (`lem:gr_transition_self`): since `P^I_I = 1` the minor
253	`X^I_I = 1` is its own Cramer inverse, so the pre-hom is the structure map and its
254	away-localisation lift is the identity ring homomorphism. Project-local. -/
255	theorem transitionMap_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
256	    transitionMap d r I I hI hI = RingHom.id (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
257	  have hmin1 : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
258	    simp only [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self]
259	    exact Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)
260	  have hinv1 : universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
261	    simp only [universalMinorInv, hmin1]
262	    exact inv_one
263	  have himg : imageMatrix d r I I hI hI = (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _) := by
264	    rw [imageMatrix, hinv1]
265	    exact Matrix.one_mul _
266	  have hpre : (transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI).toRingHom
267	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
268	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
269	    apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
270	    · intro n

10	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
11	
12	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
13	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
14	base change on the target.
15	
16	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
17	```
18	  X' --g'--> X
19	  |f'        |f
20	  v          v
21	  S' --g---> S
22	```
23	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
24	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
25	
26	The three main declarations are:
27	
28	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
29	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
30	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
31	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
32	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
33	  associativity).
34	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
35	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
36	
37	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
38	
39	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
40	Tag 02KH.
41	-/

53	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
54	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
55	
56	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
57	
58	Given a commutative square
59	```
60	  X' --g'--> X
61	  |f'        |f
62	  v          v
63	  S' --g---> S
64	```
65	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
66	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
67	
68	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
69	```
70	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
71	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
72	```
73	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
74	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
75	commutativity of the square.
76	
77	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
78	base-change diagram. -/
79	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
80	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
81	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
82	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
83	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
84	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
85	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
86	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)

625	        ((Spec.map φ).toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom).map tForget) := rfl
626	  -- The open-naturality square `ρ ≫ e₂.hom = e₁.hom ≫ Gmor` (the residual `hsq`).
627	  have hsq : ρ ≫ e₂.hom = e₁.hom ≫ Gmor := by
628	    -- Substitute the structural exposures, then unfold `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`. Here
629	    --   ρ = rsc gsR ∘ rsc pushTop ∘ tForget,  Gmor = rsc φ ∘ σmor = rsc φ ∘ rsc gsR' ∘ tForget,
630	    --   e_U = (rsc-comp')⁻¹ ≫ (restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app SecN ≫ (rsc-comp').
631	    rw [he₁, he₂, hGmor, hρ', hσ']
632	    simp only [gammaPushforwardIsoAt, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
633	    -- KEY INSIGHT (iter-241): every constituent of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` is the IDENTITY on
634	    -- underlying elements — `restrictScalarsComp'App_{hom,inv}_apply`, `restrictScalarsCongr`
635	    -- app, and `restrictScalars.map_apply` are all `rfl`; the middle `restrictScalarsCongr` (now
636	    -- replaces the former `eqToIso`, removing the only non-`rfl` cast) merely repackages the module
637	    -- structure on the unchanged carrier. So both legs of the square send `x` to the underlying
638	    -- `tForget x` definitionally — a pointwise `rfl` closes it, sidestepping the rewrite-matching
639	    -- pathology that defeated the prior `restrictScalarsComp'App_inv_naturality`-rewrite route.
640	    ext x
641	    rfl
642	  -- Solve for `ρ` and transport the localization across the two equivalences.
643	  have hρ : ρ = (e₁.hom ≫ Gmor) ≫ e₂.inv := (Iso.eq_comp_inv e₂).mpr hsq
644	  have key : ρ.hom
645	      = e₂.toLinearEquiv.symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ
646	          (Gmor.hom ∘ₗ e₁.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap) := by
647	    rw [hρ]; rfl
648	  rw [key]
649	  haveI hstep : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
650	      (Gmor.hom ∘ₗ e₁.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap) :=
651	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers a) Gmor.hom e₁.toLinearEquiv
652	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers a)
653	    (Gmor.hom ∘ₗ e₁.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap) e₂.toLinearEquiv.symm

656	
657	/-- **Naturality of the Γ-fragment comparison.** The per-object isomorphisms
658	`gammaPushforwardIso φ N : Γ_R((Spec φ)_* N) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ_{R'} N)` assemble into a
659	natural isomorphism of functors `(Spec R').Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R`
660	\[ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \;\circ\; \Gamma_R
661	   \;\cong\; \Gamma_{R'} \;\circ\; \operatorname{restr}_\varphi . \]
662	Every constituent of `gammaPushforwardIso` is the identity on underlying elements (the
663	`restrictScalarsComp'App` isos and `restrictScalarsCongr` merely repackage the module structure
664	on the unchanged carrier), so naturality is a pointwise `rfl`. This is the right-adjoint natural
665	isomorphism that drives the affine pullback dictionary `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via
666	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardNatIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
668	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ≅
669	      moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R') ⋙ ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom :=
670	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun N => gammaPushforwardIso φ N) (by
671	    intro N N' g
672	    ext x
673	    rfl)

675	/-- **Affine pullback of a tilde-module.** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and an `R`-module `M`,
676	pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` back along `Spec φ` is, up to canonical isomorphism, the
677	tilde of the extension of scalars `R' ⊗_R M`. This is the pullback companion of
678	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and is part (1) of Stacks Tag 01I9 (`lemma-widetilde-pullback`).
679	
680	The construction is the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints route. Pullback along `Spec φ` is left adjoint
681	to pushforward, and `tilde` is left adjoint to global sections; their composite
682	`tilde_R ⋙ pullback` is left adjoint to `pushforward ⋙ Γ_R`. Symmetrically `extendScalars φ` is
683	left adjoint to `restrictScalars φ`, so `extendScalars ⋙ tilde_{R'}` is left adjoint to
684	`Γ_{R'} ⋙ restrictScalars φ`. The two right adjoints are identified by the natural isomorphism
685	`gammaPushforwardNatIso` (which is the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardIso`), so
686	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso` yields an isomorphism of the two left adjoints; evaluating
687	at `M` gives the claim. Project-local: the affine companion of the pushforward dictionary. See
688	blueprint `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
689	noncomputable def pullback_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
690	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) :
691	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
692	      tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
693	  let adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
694	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map φ))
695	  let adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj φ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))
696	  (((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso φ)).symm).app M

699	
700	/-- **The pullback cone legs are the `Spec`-maps of the tensor inclusions.** For ring maps
701	`ψ : R ⟶ R'` and `φ : R ⟶ A` (regarding `A`, `R'` as `R`-algebras via `φ`, `ψ`), over the generic
702	pullback square `Limits.pullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)` Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso` identifies
703	the total space with `Spec (A ⊗[R] R')`, and under it the two cone legs are the `Spec`-maps of the
704	canonical tensor inclusions `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom : A → A ⊗[R] R'` and
705	`Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : R' → A ⊗[R] R'`. The only content beyond Mathlib's
706	`pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst`/`_inv_snd` is the structure bridging `Spec.map φ ↔ Spec.map (algebraMap
707	R A)` (definitional once `A` carries the `φ`-algebra structure). See blueprint
708	`lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`. -/
709	theorem pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) :
710	    letI : Algebra R A := φ.hom.toAlgebra
711	    letI : Algebra R R' := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
712	    ((pullbackSpecIso (R := ↑R) (S := ↑A) (T := ↑R')).inv ≫
713	        Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
714	        = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom)) ∧
715	      ((pullbackSpecIso (R := ↑R) (S := ↑A) (T := ↑R')).inv ≫
716	        Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
717	        = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
718	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := ↑R) (A := ↑A) (B := ↑R')).toRingHom)) := by
719	  letI : Algebra R A := φ.hom.toAlgebra
720	  letI : Algebra R R' := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
721	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
722	  · exact pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst ↑R ↑A ↑R'
723	  · exact pullbackSpecIso_inv_snd ↑R ↑A ↑R'

727	/-- **Domain read of the section-level base-change map.** In the affine–affine model
728	(`g = Spec ψ`, `f = Spec φ`, `F = tilde M`), the global sections of the domain
729	`g^*(f_*(tilde M))` of the base-change map are canonically `R' ⊗_R M` as an `R'`-module — i.e.
730	the extension of scalars along `ψ` of the restriction of scalars along `φ` of `M`. The iso
731	`Θ_src` is the composite of the pushforward dictionary `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (reading
732	`f_*(tilde M)` as `restrictScalars φ M`) followed by the pullback dictionary
733	`pullback_spec_tilde_iso` (reading `g^*` of a tilde as extension of scalars along `ψ`), then the
734	tilde–Γ unit. The cospan maps `f = Spec φ`, `g = Spec ψ` are genuine `Spec`-maps, so the two
735	dictionaries apply directly with no pullback-leg identification needed. See blueprint
736	`lem:base_change_mate_domain_read`. -/
737	noncomputable def base_change_mate_domain_read {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
738	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
739	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).obj
740	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).obj
741	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))) ≅
742	      (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
743	  (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).mapIso
744	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).mapIso (pushforward_spec_tilde_iso φ M) ≪≫
745	        pullback_spec_tilde_iso ψ ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M)) ≪≫
746	    (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app
747	      ((ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M))).symm
748	
749	/-- **Pullback of modules along an isomorphism of schemes is an equivalence.** Project-local
750	helper: the inverse is `pullback (inv f)`, with unit/counit assembled from `pullbackComp` and
751	`pullbackId`. Used to recognise that pulling back along `pullbackSpecIso.hom` (an iso) and pushing
752	forward again returns the original module. -/
753	noncomputable def pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsIso f] :
754	    Y.Modules ≌ X.Modules :=
755	  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk (Scheme.Modules.pullback f) (Scheme.Modules.pullback (inv f))
756	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y).symm ≪≫
757	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (IsIso.inv_hom_id f).symm ≪≫
758	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (inv f) f).symm)
759	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (inv f) ≪≫
760	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (IsIso.hom_inv_id f) ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X)
761	
762	instance pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsIso f] :
763	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).IsEquivalence :=
764	  (pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso f).isEquivalence_functor
765	
766	/-- **Codomain read of the section-level base-change map.** In the affine–affine model, with
767	`f' = pullback.snd` and `g' = pullback.fst` the legs of the pullback square, the global sections of
768	the codomain `f'_*(g')^*(tilde M)` are canonically `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` as an `R'`-module. The legs
769	are identified with the `Spec`-maps of the tensor inclusions by
770	`pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor` (L1), pulling the functors back to the affine `Spec (A ⊗_R R')`
771	chart, after which the two affine dictionaries apply. See blueprint
772	`lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read`. -/
773	noncomputable def base_change_mate_codomain_read {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
774	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
775	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
776	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
777	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).obj
778	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
779	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
780	            (tilde M))) ≅
781	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars
782	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
783	            (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom).obj
784	        ((ModuleCat.extendScalars
785	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
786	            (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M) := by
787	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
788	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
789	  set e := pullbackSpecIso (R := (R : Type u)) (S := (A : Type u)) (T := (R' : Type u)) with he
790	  set inclA := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
791	    (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))) with hinclA
792	  set inclR' := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight
793	    (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom with hinclR'
794	  obtain ⟨hfst0, hsnd0⟩ := pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor ψ φ
795	  have hfst : Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) = e.hom ≫ Spec.map inclA :=
796	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq e).mp hfst0

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).
57	
58	## Mathlib status
59	
60	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
61	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
62	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
63	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
64	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
65	- `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
66	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
67	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
68	
69	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
70	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
71	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
72	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
73	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
74	
75	## References
76	
77	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`. Source:
78	Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", §1 (FGA Explained
79	Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–35); cf. Hartshorne III.5.2.
80	-/

92	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
93	
94	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
95	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
96	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
97	polynomial is the function
98	
99	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
100	
101	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
102	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
105	III.5.2). -/
106	
107	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
108	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
109	
110	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
111	
112	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
113	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
114	
115	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
116	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
117	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
118	
119	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
120	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
121	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
122	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
123	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
124	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
125	    Polynomial ℚ :=
126	  sorry

128	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
129	
130	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
131	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
132	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
133	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
134	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
135	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
136	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
137	
138	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
139	
140	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
141	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
142	
143	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
144	
145	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
146	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
147	
148	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
149	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
150	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
151	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
152	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
153	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
154	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
155	
156	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
157	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
158	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
159	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
160	is a typed `sorry`. -/
161	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
162	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
163	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
164	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
165	  sorry
166	
167	end Scheme

169	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
170	
171	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
172	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
173	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
174	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
175	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
176	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
177	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
178	
179	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
180	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
181	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
182	
183	namespace Scheme
184	
185	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
186	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
187	
188	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
189	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
190	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
191	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
192	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
193	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
194

233	
234	These declarations build the support/freeness predicates of

241	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
242	
243	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
244	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
245	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
246	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
247	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
248	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).

250	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
251	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
252	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
253	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
254	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
255	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
256	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
257

260	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator ideal sheaf and schematic support
261	
262	These declarations build the annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules and the
263	support/properness predicates of `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`,

266	or proper-support predicate, so they are built here.
267	
268	The annihilator is packaged via `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, exactly mirroring
269	Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
270	`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
271	family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
272	internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
273	makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
274	`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
275	which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
276	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
277	the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
278	(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
279	`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/

285	/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
286	(`def:modules_annihilator`).
287	
288	On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
289	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
290	is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
291	in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
292	sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
293	the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
294	characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).
295	
296	This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
297	for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
298	noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
299	  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)
300

302	module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction

304	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
305	lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
306	    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
307	  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _

310	(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
311	ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
312	noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
313	  (annihilator F).subscheme

320	noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=

328	def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=

335	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
336	
337	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
338	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
339	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
340	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
341	annihilator commutes with localization:
342	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
343	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
344	-/
345	
346	namespace Module
347	
348	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
349	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
350	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
351	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
352	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
353	
354	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
355	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
356	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
357	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
358	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
359	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
360	
361	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
362	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
363	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
364	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
365	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
366	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
367	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
368	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
369	  classical
370	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
371	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
372	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
373	    intro r h
374	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
375	    intro x
376	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
377	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
378	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
379	    | zero => simp
380	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
381	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
382	  apply le_antisymm
383	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
384	    intro y hy
385	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
386	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
387	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
388	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
389	      intro m hm
390	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
391	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
392	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
393	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
394	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
395	    choose u hu using hgen
396	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
397	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
398	      intro m hm
399	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
400	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)
401	      have hcoe : ((∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2 : S) : R) = (u m hm : R) * (c : R) := by
402	        rw [hc]; push_cast; ring
403	      rw [hcoe, mul_smul, smul_comm, hu m hm, smul_zero]
404	    have hUa : (U : R) * a ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
405	      apply key; intro m hm; rw [mul_smul]; exact hU m hm
406	    have heq : IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ a s
407	        = (algebraMap R Rₚ ((U : R) * a)) * IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ 1 (U * s) := by
408	      rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_eq_mul_mk'_one, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq]
409	      push_cast; ring
410	    rw [heq]
411	    exact Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hUa)
412	  · -- `(Ann R M).map ⊆ Ann Rₚ Mₚ`: the image of an annihilator annihilates
413	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
414	    intro a ha
415	    rw [Ideal.mem_comap, Module.mem_annihilator]
416	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at ha
417	    intro x
418	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f x
419	    dsimp only [Function.uncurry]
420	    rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_one (M := S) Rₚ a, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, ha m,
421	      IsLocalizedModule.mk'_zero]

8	/-!
9	# Generic flatness of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file packages the classical Grothendieck/Nitsure~\S4 **generic flatness**
12	theorem: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent `𝓞_X`-module on a
13	finite-type morphism `X ⟶ S` becomes flat after restricting to a non-empty open
14	subscheme of `S`. This is the inductive engine underlying the
15	flattening-stratification construction of Grothendieck's existence theorem for
16	the Quot scheme.
17	
18	Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the *intended* substantive type
19	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
20	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a `sorry`
21	body where the proof is not yet supplied. The substantive generic-flatness
22	proof is deep (Nitsure~\S4: prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
23	clearing denominators).
24	
25	The blueprint-pinned declaration is:
26	
27	- `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem) — **generic flatness**
28	  [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf
29	  on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. Its
30	  algebraic form (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin) is the
31	  statement that a finite module over a finite-type algebra over a noetherian
32	  domain becomes free after inverting one non-zero element of the base.
33	
34	The file-internal `GenericFreeness` namespace lands the **finite-module /
35	finite-morphism special case** of the algebraic form axiom-clean, as a reusable
36	building block for the dévissage's leaves.
37	
38	## Note on type expressivity
39	
40	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
41	`genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V` and
42	substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers are necessary
43	(without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
44	
45	## Mathlib status
46	
47	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`
48	(morphism-level), `IsImmersion`, `IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`,
49	`LocallyOfFiniteType`, and the dévissage / Noether-normalisation infrastructure
50	the algebraic generic-flatness argument consumes. It does NOT yet provide the
51	polynomial-ring core of generic freeness, nor the geometric `genericFlatness`
52	statement packaging it over a noetherian integral base.
53	
54	## References
55	
56	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`.
57	Source: Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot schemes", §4 (FGA Explained
58	Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–18); Stacks Project tag 00HB (module flat).
59	-/

74	
75	The full algebraic statement — `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a *finite-type*
76	`A`-algebra, `M` a finite `B`-module ⟹ `∃ f ≠ 0` with `M_f` free over `A_f` —
77	is a deep theorem (prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
78	clearing denominators). Mathlib already supplies most of the dévissage
79	machinery (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`,

86	case**: when `M` is finite as an `A`-module (in particular when `B` is
87	module-finite over `A`), generic freeness follows directly from
88	`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` applied at the
89	generic point `Frac A`, where `M ⊗_A Frac A` is a finite vector space hence

91	theorem (the case of a *finite* morphism `X → S`), and a reusable building

96	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-module case.** For a noetherian integral domain
97	`A` and a finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that the
98	localisation `M_f` is free over `A_f = Localization.Away f`.

100	This is the `d = 0` (finite-morphism) special case of the algebraic
101	generic-flatness theorem (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4):
102	inverting the generic point `Frac A`, the localised module is a finite vector
103	space hence free, and `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`
104	descends that freeness to a single basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec A`. -/
105	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite
106	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
107	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
108	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
109	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
110	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation A M := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite A M
111	  obtain ⟨r, hr, hfree, _⟩ :=
112	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers (nonZeroDivisors A)
113	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (nonZeroDivisors A) M) (FractionRing A)
114	  exact ⟨r, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hr, hfree⟩

117	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`: for a noetherian domain `A` and a
118	finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f` with `M_f` flat over `A_f`.
119	This is the affine-local content of `genericFlatness` for a finite morphism. -/
120	theorem exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite
121	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
122	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
123	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
124	      Module.Flat (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
125	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
126	  haveI := hfree
127	  exact ⟨f, hf, Module.Flat.of_free⟩

129	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-morphism case.** If `A` is a noetherian domain,
130	`B` a *module-finite* `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module (with the
131	compatible `A`-module structure), then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `M_f`
132	free over `A_f`. Reduces to `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` via
133	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
134	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
135	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
136	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
137	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
138	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
139	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
140	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
141	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
142	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
143	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
144	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M

146	/-! ### Dévissage chain for the finite-type residue (Nitsure §4)
147	
148	The following sub-lemmas decompose the surviving residue of
149	`genericFlatnessAlgebraic` — the case where `M` is finite over the finite-type
150	`A`-algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A` — into the classical Nitsure §4
151	induction. They are ordered so each step uses only earlier blocks (or Mathlib
152	anchors). Throughout, `A` is a noetherian domain with fraction field
153	`K = Frac A`, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module with
154	the compatible `A`-module structure via the scalar tower `A → B → M`. -/
155	
156	/-- **L1 — torsion base case `M_K = 0`** (`lem:gf_torsion_base`, Nitsure §4 base
157	case of the induction). If the generic localisation `M_K = K ⊗_A M` (encoded as
158	`LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M`) is trivial and `M` is a finite
159	`B`-module, then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `f • M = 0`; consequently
160	`M_f = 0` is free over `A_f`.
161	
162	The hypothesis `Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M)` is the
163	formal encoding of `M_K = 0`: localising `M` at the non-zero divisors of the
164	domain `A` inverts exactly the non-zero elements, so `M_K = S^{-1}M` with
165	`S = A \ {0}`. Each generator of the finite `B`-module `M` is then killed by a
166	non-zero element of `A`; the product `f` of those annihilators (non-zero since
167	`A` is a domain) annihilates all of `M`, whence `M_f` is subsingleton and free. -/
168	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion
169	    (A B M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
170	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
171	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
172	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
173	    (htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M)) :
174	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
175	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
176	  -- The `A`/`B` scalar actions commute (A acts through `algebraMap A B`).
177	  have hcomm : ∀ (a : A) (b : B) (x : M), a • b • x = b • a • x := fun a b x => by
178	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a (b • x), ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a x,
179	      smul_smul, smul_smul, mul_comm]
180	  -- Torsion: every element of `M` is killed by a non-zero divisor of `A`.
181	  rw [LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff] at htors
182	  choose r hr using htors
183	  -- `r m ∈ A⁰` and `(r m) • m = 0`.
184	  -- A finite `B`-generating set of `M`.
185	  obtain ⟨S, hS⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top : (⊤ : Submodule B M).FG)
186	  -- The product of the chosen annihilators of the generators.
187	  set f : A := ∏ x ∈ S, r x with hf_def
188	  have hfmem : f ∈ nonZeroDivisors A := prod_mem fun x _ => (hr x).1
189	  have hf0 : f ≠ 0 := nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hfmem
190	  -- `f` annihilates every generator, hence all of `span B S = ⊤ = M`.
191	  have hgen : ∀ x ∈ S, f • x = (0 : M) := by
192	    intro x hx
193	    classical
194	    rw [hf_def, ← Finset.prod_erase_mul S r hx, mul_smul, (hr x).2, smul_zero]
195	  have hfM : ∀ m : M, f • m = (0 : M) := by
196	    intro m
197	    have hmem : m ∈ Submodule.span B (↑S : Set M) := by rw [hS]; exact Submodule.mem_top
198	    induction hmem using Submodule.span_induction with
199	    | mem x hx => exact hgen x hx
200	    | zero => exact smul_zero f
201	    | add x y _ _ ihx ihy => rw [smul_add, ihx, ihy, add_zero]
202	    | smul b x _ ih => rw [hcomm, ih, smul_zero]
203	  -- `f • M = 0` ⟹ `M_f` subsingleton ⟹ free over `A_f`.
204	  refine ⟨f, hf0, ?_⟩
205	  haveI : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) :=
206	    LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff.mpr fun m => ⟨f, Submonoid.mem_powers f, hfM m⟩
207	  exact Module.Free.of_subsingleton _ _

210	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact`, Nitsure §4). Given a short exact

213	exact sequence `0 → M'_f → M_f → M''_f → 0` of `A_f`-modules; the localised
214	`i_f` is injective and the localised `q_f` is surjective.
215	
216	Localisation of modules at a multiplicative set is exact, so it preserves the
217	injection `i`, the surjection `q`, and exactness at the middle term
218	(`LocalizedModule.map_injective` / `…map_surjective` / `…map_exact` applied to
219	the scalar-restricted maps). -/
220	theorem exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact
221	    (A B M' M M'' : Type*) [CommRing A]
222	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
223	    [AddCommGroup M'] [Module B M'] [Module A M'] [IsScalarTower A B M']
224	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
225	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module B M''] [Module A M''] [IsScalarTower A B M'']
226	    (i : M' →ₗ[B] M) (q : M →ₗ[B] M'')
227	    (hi : Function.Injective i) (hq : Function.Surjective q)
228	    (hexact : Function.Exact i q)
229	    (f : A) :
230	    Function.Injective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A)) ∧
231	    Function.Surjective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (q.restrictScalars A)) ∧
232	    Function.Exact (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A))
233	      (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (q.restrictScalars A)) := by
234	  refine ⟨LocalizedModule.map_injective _ (i.restrictScalars A) hi,
235	    LocalizedModule.map_surjective _ (q.restrictScalars A) hq, ?_⟩
236	  exact LocalizedModule.map_exact (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A)
